skill.name: theme-factory
skill.description: Apply curated visual themes to presentations, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts. The skill provides ten named palettes with font pairings and can help create a custom theme when the presets do not fit.
skill.summary: Apply curated color palettes and font pairings to decks, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk, but review found no executable code, shell commands, cryptography, command-and-control behavior, or prompt injection. The reported issues are false positives from Apache License URLs, markdown inline code, color hex values, and ordinary prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to standard Apache License reference links. They are documentation text and do not perform network access or data transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static license text. No code path or instruction asks the agent to fetch these URLs or send data to them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code in Skill Instructions
low_findings.description: The reported shell backtick locations in the main skill file are markdown inline code spans for file and directory names. They do not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line uses backticks only for markdown formatting around filenames or directories. There is no executable command syntax or user input interpolation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Hex Color Values
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations in theme files are markdown inline code spans around color hex values. They are design specifications, not command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited theme lines consistently contain color names and hex codes. They are static markdown content with no runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and C2 Keyword Matches in Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and C2 findings match substrings in prose, theme names, license text, and a color value. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, malware instruction, or command-and-control logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found these matches in normal design and license language. No evidence found of encryption logic, obfuscation, persistence, networking, or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent visual styling makes decks and documents look unfinished. Theme Factory provides ready palettes and font pairings for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists ten preset visual themes with names, color palettes, typography, and suggested use contexts.
Applies selected theme colors and fonts consistently across decks, documents, reports, or HTML artifacts.
Uses the included theme showcase PDF to help users compare available visual directions.
Creates a custom theme from user guidance when the preset themes do not fit the artifact.
Checks readability and contrast while preserving the selected theme identity.
limitations: It provides theme guidance and application instructions, not a rendering engine by itself.
It does not verify that requested fonts are installed in every target environment.
It does not include brand compliance rules beyond the provided palette and typography details.
It depends on the host assistant to edit the target artifact correctly.

use_cases.title: Polish a Client Deck
use_cases.description: Apply a cohesive visual system to a proposal deck before review or delivery.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Reports
use_cases.description: Use a consistent palette and font pairing across recurring reports and status documents.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Generate a custom theme direction for launch materials, landing pages, and presentation artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Preset Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the available Theme Factory themes and recommend three options for a professional investor presentation.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Ocean Depths theme to this deck. Keep the existing content, improve contrast, and make headings consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Match a Brand Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a theme for a healthcare analytics report using calm colors, strong readability, and a modern typography pairing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Retheme an Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this artifact for inconsistent styling, select the best Theme Factory theme, apply it, and explain the main visual changes.

output_examples.input: Apply a calm professional theme to a financial report.
output_examples.output: The artifact uses Ocean Depths with deep navy section headers, teal emphasis elements, seafoam secondary accents, and clean sans-serif typography.

output_examples.input: Create a visual direction for a boutique interior design proposal.
output_examples.output: A Desert Rose direction is recommended with dusty rose accents, clay supporting tones, warm neutral backgrounds, and rich contrast for key headings.

output_examples.input: Make this launch deck feel more technical and high energy.
output_examples.output: The deck is rethemed with Tech Innovation using electric blue accents, dark high-contrast sections, bright highlights, and modern sans-serif headings.

best_practices: Review the theme showcase before selecting a visual direction for important artifacts.
Provide the artifact type, audience, and desired tone before asking for a custom theme.
Check readability after applying strong colors, especially on dense slides or tables.
anti_patterns: Do not mix several preset themes in one artifact unless sections need clear separation.
Do not apply decorative colors to critical data labels without checking contrast.
Do not request exact brand compliance without providing the required brand rules.

faq.question: What does Theme Factory do?
faq.answer: It helps apply curated color palettes and font pairings to decks, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts.

faq.question: How many preset themes are included?
faq.answer: The skill includes ten preset themes, each with colors, typography, and recommended use contexts.

faq.question: Can it create a custom theme?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create a new theme from user goals, audience details, and visual preferences.

faq.question: Does it edit files automatically?
faq.answer: It gives theme instructions to the host assistant, which must perform any actual artifact edits.

faq.question: Is it only for slide decks?
faq.answer: No. It can guide styling for slides, documents, reports, landing pages, and similar artifacts.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill content is static markdown and a showcase PDF, with no network behavior found.
